steps.title: Margin of error depends only on sample size
steps.content:
The margin of error formula is $$ME = \frac{z \cdot \sigma}{\sqrt{n}}$$ where $z$ is the critical value, $\sigma$ is the standard deviation, and $n$ is the sample size.

Since both samples were calculated the same way, they have the same $z$ and $\sigma$.

Therefore, margin of error depends only on the sample size $n$.

steps.title: Apply the inverse relationship
steps.content:
Margin of error is inversely proportional to $\sqrt{n}$: a larger sample size produces a smaller margin of error.

Sample 1's margin of error (1 minute) is smaller than Sample 2's (2.2 minutes), so Sample 1 had the larger sample size.

The first sample contained more ovens than the second sample.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
